description: Files and directories the agent can NEVER modify via the edit/write/delete_file tools. These are the engine core — if they break, the agent can't run to fix itself. A trailing slash means the whole directory tree is protected. (self_edit is a separate, gated path and may touch these.)

reason.src/chat-ws/: WebSocket layer — breaks = can't talk to agent
reason.src/canonical-loop/: Consolidated agent execution loop — breaks = can't think (replaced the old agent-providers/agent-request monoliths)
reason.src/agent-request/: Request pipeline — breaks = can't process messages
reason.src/tool-execution/: Tool dispatch pipeline — breaks = can't use tools
reason.src/tool-executor.ts: Tool runner entrypoint — breaks = can't use tools
reason.src/anthropic-client/: Claude API client — breaks = can't reach LLM
reason.src/anthropic-models.ts: Model registry — breaks = can't pick a model
reason.src/codex-client/: Codex/OpenAI-compat client — breaks = can't reach that provider
reason.src/self-edit/sandbox.ts: Self-edit safety gate — agent breaking this would brick the safety net
reason.src/self-edit/rollback.ts: Self-edit crashed-merge auto-revert — breaks = a bad merge can't be rolled back
reason.src/self-edit/: Self-edit pipeline (surgeon, exfil scan, gates) — a self_edit that rewrites its own gate walks past every later review
reason.src/update-pipeline.ts: Update validation pipeline — gates every platform update before it lands; weakening it lets unvalidated code overwrite the install
reason.src/ota-update.ts: Tarball update integrity (commit pinning, checksum verify, validation hook) — breaks = unverified bytes over the install
reason.src/settings.ts: Settings reader — developer_mode and the security kill-switches resolve through here; a rewritten reader can fake any protected setting
reason.src/routes/connector-proxy.ts: Connector proxy — attaches vault secrets to outbound requests under manifest allow-lists; a weakened matcher leaks credentials
reason.src/agency/worktree.ts: Worktree manager barrel — the self_edit merge machinery's public API
reason.src/agency/worktree-core.ts: Worktree git runner + registry — controls every git op a self_edit performs
reason.src/agency/worktree-state.ts: Self_edit merge-gate predicates (security-hold / deps / merge-delta) — the gate could otherwise rewrite its own check
reason.src/agency/worktree-lifecycle.ts: Worktree create/merge/cleanup — the step that actually lands a self_edit on main
reason.src/agency/worktree-junctions.ts: node_modules junction teardown + orphan sweep — breaks = a merge can corrupt the parent deps
reason.src/tool-policy/: Tool capability policy — breaks = tools escape their declared class
reason.src/auth/: Auth + OAuth — breaks = locked out / can't authenticate
reason.src/config-loader.ts: Config file reader — breaks = can't load config
reason.src/context-manager/: Context window management — breaks = broken long conversations
reason.src/types.ts: Type definitions — breaks = compile errors everywhere
reason.src/ari-kernel/: ARI kernel — breaks = no policy enforcement
reason.config/protected-files.json: This file — agent can't remove its own guardrails
reason.config/system-prompt.md: Global agent prompt. Per-user preferences and facts go to MEMORY, not here — use `remember`, `memory_set_user_field`, or `memory_update_profile` instead. Editing the system prompt for user-specific content pollutes every future conversation with every user.
